meaning: 都灵[意大利西北部城市](即托里诺
examples: He hung them above the same table he had moved from Turin.
"Nicolo," whose real name cannot be exposed to the public because of Italy's privacy laws, finished working the whole night at a factory in Turin.
Something was as noticeable in cafés as in the factories of Milan and Turin.
It was like watching Sasha Cohen take a header at Turin in the 2006 Olympic Games.
But Turin and Sanchez understand that chemistry alone does not determine the success of a perfume.
